answer: In Manusmriti chapter 8, the penalty for a non-Brahmin (low-born man) who strikes a Brahmin (or any superior) is: whatever limb he used to injure the superior shall be cut off — this is stated as the general rule in Mn_8.279. Specifically, Mn_8.280 elaborates: if he raises his hand or a stick against a superior, his hand shall be cut off; if he strikes with his foot, his foot shall be cut off. These verses are found in work 'unknown-manusmrti-347b76', chapter 8, paragraphs p-526528 (Mn_8.279) and p-c0dabc (Mn_8.280).
